title: Action
description: Schema for action JSON files that define actions for classes. Action files are located alongside their class file with '.actions.json' extension (e.g., 'order_management/order.actions.json' for class 'order_management/order.class.json'). Actions represent operations that modify state and are referenced by state machines. The file contains a map of action keys to action definitions. If validation fails, verify 'name' is present and non-empty for each action.

name.description: The display name of the action (e.g., 'Send Confirmation Email', 'Reserve Inventory', 'Calculate Total'). This is the human-readable name shown in state diagrams and documentation. Use verb phrases that describe what the action does. Must be non-empty. If validation fails: ensure this is a string with at least one character.

details.description: Optional detailed description of the action. Describe what the action does, what side effects it has, and any important implementation considerations. This helps implementers understand the full scope of the action.

parameters.description: Parameters for the action. Each parameter has a name and optional data type rules.

data_type_rules.description: Data type rules for the parameter value.

requires.description: Preconditions that must be true before the action can execute. Each item is a logic specification with a description and optional formal notation. Specifications must not contain primed values (primes are only allowed in safety_rules). Items may also be 'let' bindings that define local variables for use by subsequent items.

type.description: Optional logic kind discriminator. Use 'let' for local variable definitions.

description.description: A precondition statement. Describe what must be true for this action to execute correctly.

target.description: Required for 'let' type: the local variable name. Must not start with '_'. Must be omitted or empty for 'assessment' type.

target_type_spec.description: Optional TLA+ type declaration for the target (e.g., 'Int', 'STRING'). Only meaningful for 'let' type.

notation.description: Optional formal notation system. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus' (TLA+ notation). If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation. Must not contain primed values (primes are only allowed in safety_rules).

guarantees.description: Postconditions that will be true after the action executes successfully. Each guarantee sets one attribute via its 'target' field (the lowercase attribute SubKey). The target separates the left-hand side so the specification contains only the right-hand-side value expression with no primed values (e.g., for 'self.log' = Append(self.log, amount)', use target='log' and specification='Append(self.log, amount)'). Each attribute can only be set once per action. Items may also be 'let' bindings that define local variables for use by subsequent items.

type.description: Optional logic kind discriminator. Use 'let' for local variable definitions.

description.description: A postcondition statement. Describe what will be true after the action completes.

target.description: Required. For 'state_change': the lowercase attribute SubKey being set (e.g., 'shipping', 'status'). For 'let': the local variable name. Must not start with '_'.

target_type_spec.description: Optional TLA+ type declaration for the target (e.g., 'Int', 'STRING'). Only meaningful for 'let' type.

notation.description: Optional formal notation system. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus' (TLA+ notation). If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation. This must be only the right-hand-side value expression with no primed values. The left-hand side is captured by the 'target' field. For example, for 'self.log' = Append(self.log, amount)', use target='log' and specification='Append(self.log, amount)'.

safety_rules.description: Safety rules that must always hold during and after the action. Each item is a boolean constraint that MAY reference primed values (e.g., 'inventory' >= 0') to express constraints over both prior and new state. This is the only logic context where primed values are allowed in specifications. Items may also be 'let' bindings that define local variables for use by subsequent items.

type.description: Optional logic kind discriminator. Use 'let' for local variable definitions.

target.description: Required for 'let' type: the local variable name. Must not start with '_'. Must be omitted or empty for 'safety_rule' type.

target_type_spec.description: Optional TLA+ type declaration for the target (e.g., 'Int', 'STRING'). Only meaningful for 'let' type.

notation.description: Optional formal notation system. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus' (TLA+ notation). If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation. Safety rule specifications MAY contain primed values (e.g., 'inventory' >= 0') to express constraints over both prior and new state.
